summary: react-ui-polyfills executes remote code from anonymous paste host
details: The package advertises itself as a React polyfill / SVG sanitizer but the only exported function, `getPlugin()`, returns a callable that fetches https://jsonkeeper.com/b/GMIHS (an anonymous, mutable JSON paste host) over HTTPS and passes the response's `cookie` field directly to `eval()`. There is no integrity check, no version pin, and no disclosure of network or eval behavior in the README. Any consumer that invokes the returned function executes whatever code the attacker currently hosts at that URL inside the consumer's Node process. The package name and security-themed marketing copy ('never trust SVG input', 'prevent XSS') appear designed to attract developers searching for sanitization utilities, while the implementation performs no SVG work at all.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
